content.body: That's _technically_ true in certain ways but in practice every large-ish company is using one of the many non-npm proxies or alternate (internal) registries.
content.formatted_body: That's technically true in certain ways but in practice every large-ish company is using one of the many non-npm proxies or alternate (internal) registries.

content.body: sure, but all of them follow npm
(and nobody uses yarn > 1, and yarn 1 is many years EOL - so we're really just talking about pnpm)

"sure, but all of them follow npm" - yes, they all try to reverse engineer the responses from the npm registry and try their best to patch the same API. to be clear, I'm talking less about the clients and a little more about the servers (jfrog, GAR, etc.)

content.body: that puts you in a very tiny minority
i think it would be amazing to make a registry-agnostic "test suite" that ensured a registry worked in a "standard" format
